confidence.disclosure: Reconstructed from the surviving Muruj al-Dhahab (947 recension) and Kitab al-Tanbih wa-l-Ishraf (956), supplemented by Ahmad Shboul's Al-Mas'udi and his World (1979). Most dates are inferred from internal references; al-Masudi is rarely precise about the year of any individual visit. The famous claim that he reached Madagascar and southern China is treated cautiously by Shboul and is omitted here in favour of stops he firmly attests visiting in person.

sources: Al-Masudi, Muruj al-Dhahab wa Ma'adin al-Jawhar (947)
Ahmad M.H. Shboul, Al-Mas'udi and his World (1979)
Paul Lunde and Caroline Stone (trans.), The Meadows of Gold: The Abbasids (1989)

journey.event: Born in Baghdad, descendant of the Companion Abdullah ibn Mas'ud.
Began his travels from Baghdad via Basra, the principal port for the Indian Ocean.
Travelled through Fars, recording the ruins of the Achaemenid capital.
Reached the Indus valley, recording the great solar temple of Multan.
Visited the Gujarati port of Cambay, comparing its tidal estuary to the Nile and the Tigris.
Recorded the Konkan port of Saymur and its mixed Muslim-Hindu population.
Reached Sarandib (Sri Lanka), confirming the southern monsoon route.
Sailed back through Oman, recording its pearl divers and date palms.
Reached the East African 'land of the Zanj' where he recorded local religion and language; Qanbalu is most often identified with Pemba.
Returned via the Omani coast and Sohar to the Persian Gulf.
Returned to Baghdad and spent several years studying with the Mu'tazilite scholars of the city.
Travelled along the Tigris recording its tributaries and trade.
Visited the Caspian shore of northern Iran during the Buyid expansion.
Travelled through the Caucasus, recording Khazars, Alans and other peoples of the steppe.
Reached Hamdanid Aleppo and met the Byzantine convert Leo of Tripoli.
Visited the frontier with Byzantium, gathering material on the Romans.
Recorded Damascus during his decade in Syria and Palestine.
Visited the Lake Tiberias region, recording the local Jewish and Christian communities.
Returned to the Byzantine frontier, where he obtained an Andalusian bishop's list of Frankish kings from Clovis to Louis IV.
Settled in Fustat, where he completed the surviving 947 recension of Muruj al-Dhahab.
Completed Kitab al-Tanbih wa-l-Ishraf in Fustat shortly before his death in September 956.
